challenges.title: Menu Structure Builder
challenges.description: Create a dictionary called 'menu_structure' that represents a menu bar. It should map menu names to lists of item names: 'File' -> ['New', 'Open', 'Save', 'Exit'], 'Edit' -> ['Cut', 'Copy', 'Paste', 'Undo'], 'Help' -> ['About', 'Documentation']. Then write a function 'get_menu_items' that takes the menu dict and a menu name, returning the list of items or an empty list if the menu doesn't exist.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'File' in menu_structure
assert 'Edit' in menu_structure
assert 'Help' in menu_structure
assert menu_structure['File'] == ['New', 'Open', 'Save', 'Exit']
assert menu_structure['Edit'] == ['Cut', 'Copy', 'Paste', 'Undo']
assert menu_structure['Help'] == ['About', 'Documentation']
assert get_menu_items(menu_structure, 'File') == ['New', 'Open', 'Save', 'Exit']
assert get_menu_items(menu_structure, 'Edit') == ['Cut', 'Copy', 'Paste', 'Undo']
assert get_menu_items(menu_structure, 'View') == []
assert get_menu_items({}, 'File') == []
hints: Build the dict with exact keys and list values as described.
Use menu.get(name, []) to return an empty list for missing menus.

challenges.title: Canvas Coordinate Tracker
challenges.description: Write a class called 'CoordinateTracker' that tracks named points on a 2D canvas. It should support: add_point(name, x, y) — store a point; get_point(name) — return (x, y) tuple or None; move_point(name, dx, dy) — shift a point by dx, dy (raise KeyError if not found); distance(name1, name2) — return Euclidean distance between two points (raise KeyError if either not found); all_points() — return dict of {name: (x, y)}.
challenges.test_code:
ct = CoordinateTracker()
ct.add_point('origin', 0, 0)
ct.add_point('right', 3, 0)
ct.add_point('up', 0, 4)

assert ct.get_point('origin') == (0, 0)
assert ct.get_point('right') == (3, 0)
assert ct.get_point('missing') is None

# Distance
assert ct.distance('origin', 'right') == 3.0
assert ct.distance('origin', 'up') == 4.0
assert ct.distance('right', 'up') == 5.0

# Move
ct.move_point('origin', 1, 1)
assert ct.get_point('origin') == (1, 1)
ct.move_point('origin', -1, -1)
assert ct.get_point('origin') == (0, 0)

# All points
all_pts = ct.all_points()
assert len(all_pts) == 3
assert all_pts['right'] == (3, 0)

# KeyError on missing
try:
    ct.move_point('nope', 1, 1)
    assert False, 'Should raise KeyError'
except KeyError:
    pass

try:
    ct.distance('origin', 'nope')
    assert False, 'Should raise KeyError'
except KeyError:
    pass
hints: Store points in a dict: self._points = {}. Each value is a (x, y) tuple.
Euclidean distance: math.sqrt((x2-x1)**2 + (y2-y1)**2). For move_point, update the stored tuple.
